title: [lora_l2.c:379-387] lora_modem_config.tx=true set but not used for half-duplex
description:
Gap found: LoRa modem configuration not reviewed for half-duplex semantics.

Why this was missed: Config struct considered straightforward.

The actual bug: The lora_modem_config at lines 379-387 sets '.tx = true'. According to Zephyr's lora.h, this flag indicates the radio should be configured for TX mode.

For half-duplex LoRa operation:
- Set tx=true before TX
- Radio switches to RX mode for lora_recv()

The LICHEN code sets tx=true once at start() and then expects both TX and RX to work. This works because:
1. lora_send() configures TX mode internally
2. lora_recv() configures RX mode internally

The '.tx = true' in the config is just the INITIAL state preference, not a lock.

Verified correct. The Zephyr LoRa driver handles mode switching. Priority 4 - documentation note.
close_reason: Fixed: Added documentation note explaining half-duplex operation - .tx=true sets INITIAL state only, Zephyr's LoRa driver handles TX/RX mode switching internally. Both TX and RX work regardless of this setting.
